---
title: AI-Driven Analytics for Energy-Efficient and Cost-Aware Data Center Planning
format:
  poster-typst: 
    size: "36x24"
    poster-authors: "Pouya Zolfi"
    departments: "Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering"
    institution-logo: "./images/Marquette-engineering-Logo.png"
    footer-text: "Marquette University"
    footer-url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/pouyazolfi"
    footer-emails: "pouya.zolfi@marquette.edu"
    footer-color: "ebcfb2"
bibliography: ref.bib
csl: apa.csl
citeproc: true
---

```{=typst}
#set text(font: "Helvetica")
```

# Background

- Data centers consume large amounts of energy for both computing and cooling, making energy efficiency a major challenge.

- Operators must balance workload demand, electricity cost, cooling needs, and carbon emissions while maintaining reliable performance.

- This study combines descriptive analytics and AI modeling to support efficient, cost-aware, and sustainable data center planning.


# Dataset Overview

- The dataset includes hourly workload demand, electricity prices, server parameters, and ADS cooling-system states.

- These variables support integrated analysis of both computing demand and cooling-system performance under changing operating conditions.

- The dataset supports analysis of:
  - power demand
  - operating cost
  - carbon emissions
  - cooling performance

- It also provides the foundation for AI-based modeling of ADS efficiency and cooling capacity.

# Methodology


```{=typst}
#align(center,
  image("images/Method.png", width: 100%)
)
```



# Key Equations

$$
$$

```{=typst}
#align(center,
  image("images/formulas.png", width: 90%)
)
```


# Workload and Electricity Price

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_1_Hourly_Workload.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 1.* Daily workload pattern.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_2_Electricity_Price.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 2.* Hourly electricity price.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Key Insight:* Workload and price peaks do not fully align, creating opportunities for cost-aware scheduling of flexible demand.
]
```

# Power, Cost, and Carbon Results

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_3_Power_Breakdown.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 3.* Facility power breakdown.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_4_Cost_and_Carbon.svg", width: 6.6in)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 4.* Cost and carbon emissions.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Observation:* Higher workload increases both IT and cooling demand, which raises cost and carbon emissions.
]
```

# Cooling-System Performance Maps

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_5_ADS_COP_Heatmap.svg", width: 100%)
    )

    #align(center)[
      *Figure 5.* ADS COP map.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_6_ADS_Capacity_Heatmap.svg", width: 100%)
    )

    #align(center)[
      *Figure 6.* ADS capacity map.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Key Finding:* Maximum efficiency and capacity occur in different operating regions.
]
```

```{=typst}
#v(-1em)
```

# AI Model Validation

```{=typst}
#grid(
  columns: (1fr, 1fr),
  gutter: 8pt,
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_7_AI_Validation_COP.svg", width: 100%)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 7.* AI validation for COP.
    ]
  ],
  [
    #align(center,
      image("Data Center images/Figure_8_AI_Validation_Capacity.svg", width: 100%)
    )
    
    #align(center)[
      *Figure 8.* AI validation for capacity.
    ]
  ]
)
```

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  *Model Result:* The Random Forest surrogate predicts both COP and cooling capacity accurately and can support optimization and control.
]
```


# Conclusion

```{=typst}
#block(
  inset: 7pt,
  radius: 5pt,
)[
  • Workload and price profiles reveal scheduling opportunities  
  
  • Facility power, cost, and emissions vary substantially over time  
  
  • ADS performance is strongly nonlinear  
  
  • A Random Forest surrogate accurately predicts COP and cooling capacity
]
```
